code: @Manual{,   title = {data.sketches: Probabilistic Streaming Data Sketches},   author = {Pedro Baltazar},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://github.com/pedrobtz/data.sketches}, }

title: Probabilistic Streaming Data Sketches
text: data.sketches provides R interface Apache DataSketches library streaming algorithms approximate analytics data large hold process exactly. Sketches compact, mergeable summaries answer queries approximate distinct counts, quantiles ranks, frequent items point-frequency estimates, weighted sampling, set membership, within known error bounds. package implements, grouped family: Quantile sketches approximate quantiles, ranks, CDF, PMF: kll_doubles(), kll_floats() – Karnin-Lang-Liberty (KLL) sketches. req() – Relative Error Quantiles (REQ), accurate near one tail. tdigest_double() – t-Digest, accurate near tails. Cardinality sketches approximate distinct counting: hll() – HyperLogLog (HLL). cpc() – Compressed Probabilistic Counting (CPC). theta() – Theta, set operations theta_union(), theta_intersection(), theta_difference(), theta_jaccard(). Frequency sketches approximate frequency estimation: frequent_items() – frequent items (heavy hitters) character stream. count_min() – point estimates item frequency. Tuple sketches, Theta-extension pairs per-key value arrays approximate distinct counting: array_of_doubles() – Array Doubles, set operations array_of_doubles_union(), array_of_doubles_intersection(), array_of_doubles_difference(). Sampling sketches weighted sampling stream: varopt() – VarOpt, minimum-variance subset-sum estimation. ebpps() – EBPPS (Exact Bounded Probabilistic Proportional--Size), modern alternative reservoir sampling. Filters approximate set membership: bloom_filter() – Bloom filter.

title: Probabilistic Streaming Data Sketches
text: Install development version GitHub pak: install released version CRAN :
code: pak::pak("pedrobtz/data.sketches") install.packages("data.sketches")

title: Probabilistic Streaming Data Sketches
text: Build KLL sketch numeric vector query approximate quantiles ranks: Sketches mergeable, partial sketches built different chunks stream can combined one: Sketches can serialized raw vector restored, interoperability Apache DataSketches implementations:
code: library(data.sketches)  sketch <- kll_doubles(rnorm(10000)) sketch  sketch$quantile(c(0.25, 0.5, 0.75)) sketch$rank(c(-1, 0, 1)) a <- kll_doubles(rnorm(5000, mean = -2)) b <- kll_doubles(rnorm(5000, mean = 2)) a$merge(b) a$quantile(c(0.25, 0.5, 0.75)) bytes <- sketch$serialize() restored <- kll_doubles(bytes = bytes) restored$quantile(0.5)

what: Array of Doubles (Tuple) sketch for estimating sums alongside distinct counts — array_of_doubles
title: Array of Doubles (Tuple) sketch for estimating sums alongside distinct counts — array_of_doubles
text: Creates Array Doubles sketch, Tuple sketch extends theta() sketch associating fixed-length array num_values doubles retained key. estimates number distinct keys ($estimate(), theta()) also sum value column full input stream ($column_sums()), e.g. estimate total numeric measure across distinct users.

title: Array of Doubles (Tuple) sketch for estimating sums alongside distinct counts — array_of_doubles
code: array_of_doubles(   x = NULL,   values = NULL,   lg_k = NULL,   num_values = NULL,   seed = NULL,   bytes = NULL )

title: Array of Doubles (Tuple) sketch for estimating sums alongside distinct counts — array_of_doubles
text: x Optional numeric character vector keys update new sketch . element hashed contributes distinct-count estimate. values Optional value(s) associated element x: numeric vector (num_values == 1) numeric matrix num_values columns, recycled length(x) rows single value/row supplied. Defaults 1s ($column_sums() estimates count key, like $estimate()). set without x. lg_k log2 nominal number entries, single whole number [5, 26]. Larger lg_k accurate larger. Defaults 12 (resolved fresh sketch built). Must set bytes supplied. num_values Number double values associated retained key, single whole number [1, 255]. Defaults 1. Must set bytes supplied. seed Hash seed, single non-negative whole number 2^53. Defaults 9001 (upstream default), resolved whether bytes supplied. bytes Optional raw vector holding native serialized sketch reconstruct. result always compact sketch.

title: Array of Doubles (Tuple) sketch for estimating sums alongside distinct counts — array_of_doubles
text: array_of_doubles_sketch object. Key methods: $update(x, values = NULL) Add keys associated values (mutates, returns sketch). Errors sketch compact. $merge() Absorb another sketch seed num_values, becoming compact (mutates, returns sketch). $estimate() Approximate number distinct keys seen. $lower_bound(num_std_dev = 1) / $upper_bound(num_std_dev = 1) Approximate confidence bounds estimate(), 1, 2, 3 standard deviations. $column_sums() Estimated sum value column full input stream. $lg_k(), $num_values(), $seed(), $theta(), $num_retained(), $is_empty(), $is_estimation_mode(), $is_ordered(), $is_compact() Metadata accessors. $summary(), $inspect(), $serialize() Structured metadata, verbose debug output, native byte payload.

title: Array of Doubles (Tuple) sketch for estimating sums alongside distinct counts — array_of_doubles
text: one x bytes may supplied: Pass x build sketch immediately update numeric character vector keys (optionally values). Pass bytes reconstruct sketch native serialized payload (produced sketch$serialize()). result always compact sketch (see ); lg_k num_values must supplied alongside bytes. Unlike lg_k, hash seed stored payload must supplied original sketch use default. Pass neither empty (mutable) sketch given lg_k, num_values, seed. update() silently ignores NA/NaN/NA_character_ x (corresponding row values), matching missing-value policy used across families; na_rm argument. Array Doubles sketch either update sketch (mutable, $is_compact() FALSE) compact sketch (immutable, $is_compact() TRUE). Fresh sketches built x/lg_k update sketches can grown $update(). Compact sketches arise bytes = reconstruction, $merge(), array_of_doubles_*() set operations, updated . $lg_k() defined update sketches. Two sketches can merged $merge(), combined array_of_doubles_union() / array_of_doubles_intersection(), share seed (mismatch raises datasketches_seed_mismatch) num_values (mismatch raises datasketches_incompatible_sketch). Value arrays matching keys combined element-wise sum. $merge() mutates receiver compact sketch holding union inputs (can longer $update()d afterward).

title: Array of Doubles (Tuple) sketch for estimating sums alongside distinct counts — array_of_doubles
code: keys <- sample(1000, 5000, replace = TRUE) values <- runif(length(keys)) sketch <- array_of_doubles(keys, values) sketch$estimate() #> [1] 994 sketch$column_sums() #> [1] 2526.101  # Round-trip through the native byte format (always compact). restored <- array_of_doubles(bytes = sketch$serialize()) restored$is_compact() #> [1] TRUE identical(restored$column_sums(), sketch$column_sums()) #> [1] TRUE

what: Array of Doubles sketch set operations — array_of_doubles_set_operations
title: Array of Doubles sketch set operations — array_of_doubles_set_operations
text: Combine two array_of_doubles() sketches new compact array_of_doubles_sketch result, without mutating either input. b must Array Doubles sketches created seed (mismatch raises datasketches_seed_mismatch).

title: Array of Doubles sketch set operations — array_of_doubles_set_operations
code: array_of_doubles_union(a, b, lg_k = NULL)  array_of_doubles_intersection(a, b)  array_of_doubles_difference(a, b)

title: Array of Doubles sketch set operations — array_of_doubles_set_operations
text: , b array_of_doubles_sketch objects created seed. lg_k array_of_doubles_union(), log2 nominal number entries union's internal sketch, single whole number [5, 26]. Defaults larger b's configured lg_k (12 compact inputs).

title: Array of Doubles sketch set operations — array_of_doubles_set_operations
text: compact array_of_doubles_sketch object.

title: Array of Doubles sketch set operations — array_of_doubles_set_operations
text: array_of_doubles_union(, b) estimates size union union(, B). b must also share num_values (mismatch raises datasketches_incompatible_sketch); value arrays matching keys combined element-wise sum. array_of_doubles_intersection(, b) estimates size intersection intersection(, B), num_values requirement combining rule array_of_doubles_union(). array_of_doubles_difference(, b) estimates size set difference \\ B (elements B), retaining 's value arrays unchanged retained keys.

title: Array of Doubles sketch set operations — array_of_doubles_set_operations
code: a <- array_of_doubles(1:1000, runif(1000)) b <- array_of_doubles(501:1500, runif(1000)) array_of_doubles_union(a, b)$column_sums() #> [1] 1005.581 array_of_doubles_intersection(a, b)$estimate() #> [1] 500 array_of_doubles_difference(a, b)$estimate() #> [1] 500

what: Bloom filter for approximate set membership — bloom_filter
title: Bloom filter for approximate set membership — bloom_filter
text: Creates Bloom filter, probabilistic data structure approximate set membership. Querying item added always returns TRUE (false negatives); querying item never added may return TRUE probability configured false-positive probability.

title: Bloom filter for approximate set membership — bloom_filter
code: bloom_filter(   x = NULL,   max_items = NULL,   fpp = NULL,   num_bits = NULL,   num_hashes = NULL,   seed = NULL,   bytes = NULL )

title: Bloom filter for approximate set membership — bloom_filter
text: x Optional numeric character vector items update new filter . max_items Target maximum number distinct items, single positive whole number 2^53. Must supplied together fpp, combined num_bits/num_hashes. Must set bytes supplied. fpp Target false-positive probability, single number (0, 1]. Must supplied together max_items. Must set bytes supplied. num_bits Number bits filter, single positive whole number 2^53. Must supplied together num_hashes, combined max_items/fpp. Must set bytes supplied. num_hashes Number hash functions applied per item, single whole number [1, 65535]. Must supplied together num_bits. Must set bytes supplied. seed Hash seed, single non-negative whole number 2^53. Defaults 9001. Two filters can combined seed (num_hashes capacity) match. Must set bytes supplied. bytes Optional raw vector holding native serialized filter reconstruct.

title: Bloom filter for approximate set membership — bloom_filter
text: bloom_filter object. Key methods: $update(x) Add items (mutates, returns filter). $query(x) Logical vector: might element seen? $query_and_update(x) $query() prior state, $update() (mutates, returns query result). $merge() -place logical compatible filter (mutates, returns filter). $intersect() -place logical compatible filter (mutates, returns filter). $invert() -place logical (mutates, returns filter). $reset() Clear bits, keeping sizing seed (mutates, returns filter). $is_compatible() Whether may combined filter. $capacity(), $num_hashes(), $seed(), $bits_used(), $is_empty() Metadata accessors. $summary(), $inspect(), $serialize() Structured metadata, verbose debug output, native byte payload.

title: Bloom filter for approximate set membership — bloom_filter
text: Unlike sketch families, Bloom filter sub-linear size: sized front resize . two sizing strategies, combined: max_items fpp size filter target number distinct items target false-positive probability. num_bits num_hashes size filter explicitly. neither strategy specified, filter defaults max_items = 10000 fpp = 0.01. one x bytes may supplied: Pass x build filter immediately update numeric character vector items. Pass bytes reconstruct filter native serialized payload (produced filter$serialize()). max_items, fpp, num_bits, num_hashes, seed must supplied alongside bytes; restored payload. Pass neither empty (mutable) filter given sizing. update(), query(), query_and_update() silently ignore (return NA ) NA/NaN/NA_character_ x, matching missing-value policy used across families. Two filters can combined $merge() (logical ) $intersect() (logical ) "compatible": share seed, num_hashes, capacity (mismatch raises datasketches_incompatible_sketch).

title: Bloom filter for approximate set membership — bloom_filter
code: bf <- bloom_filter(letters, max_items = 1000, fpp = 0.01) bf$query(c("a", "z", "!")) #> [1]  TRUE  TRUE FALSE  # Round-trip through the native byte format. restored <- bloom_filter(bytes = bf$serialize()) restored$query("a") #> [1] TRUE

what: Suggest Bloom filter sizing parameters — bloom_filter_suggest
title: Suggest Bloom filter sizing parameters — bloom_filter_suggest
text: Helpers translate target accuracy Bloom filter constructor arguments num_bits/num_hashes sizing strategy. compute values bloom_filter() uses internally max_items/fpp sizing strategy, callers want inspect reuse (example, create multiple compatible filters explicit seed).

title: Suggest Bloom filter sizing parameters — bloom_filter_suggest
code: bloom_filter_suggest_num_filter_bits(max_items, fpp)  bloom_filter_suggest_num_hashes(max_items, num_bits)

title: Suggest Bloom filter sizing parameters — bloom_filter_suggest
text: max_items Target maximum number distinct items, single positive whole number 2^53. fpp Target false-positive probability, single number (0, 1]. num_bits Number bits filter, single positive whole number 2^53.

title: Suggest Bloom filter sizing parameters — bloom_filter_suggest
text: single number: bloom_filter_suggest_num_filter_bits() returns suggested num_bits (double, may exceed .Machine$integer.max); bloom_filter_suggest_num_hashes() returns suggested num_hashes (integer).

title: Suggest Bloom filter sizing parameters — bloom_filter_suggest
code: num_bits <- bloom_filter_suggest_num_filter_bits(1000, 0.01) num_hashes <- bloom_filter_suggest_num_hashes(1000, num_bits) bf <- bloom_filter(num_bits = num_bits, num_hashes = num_hashes)

what: Count-Min sketch for approximate point-frequency estimation — count_min
title: Count-Min sketch for approximate point-frequency estimation — count_min
text: Creates Count-Min sketch, mergeable summary estimates frequency (sum weights) individual items numeric character stream far larger memory, one-sided error: $estimate() never -estimates true frequency.

title: Count-Min sketch for approximate point-frequency estimation — count_min
code: count_min(   x = NULL,   weight = NULL,   num_hashes = NULL,   num_buckets = NULL,   seed = NULL,   bytes = NULL )

title: Count-Min sketch for approximate point-frequency estimation — count_min
text: x Optional numeric character vector update new sketch . weight Optional weight(s) x: single finite number (recycled, may negative fractional), vector values matching length x. Defaults 1 (occurrence counts ). set without x. num_hashes Number hash functions, single whole number [1, 255]. Larger values increase confidence also memory use. Defaults 3. Must set bytes supplied. See count_min_suggest_num_hashes(). num_buckets Number buckets per hash function, single whole number least 3 (num_buckets * num_hashes < 2^30). Larger values accurate larger. Defaults 55. Must set bytes supplied. See count_min_suggest_num_buckets(). seed Hash seed, single non-negative whole number 2^53. Defaults 9001. Two sketches can merged seed (num_hashes num_buckets) match. bytes Optional raw vector holding native serialized sketch reconstruct.

title: Count-Min sketch for approximate point-frequency estimation — count_min
text: count_min_sketch object. Key methods: $update(x, weight = NULL) Add numeric character values optional weight (mutates, returns sketch). $merge() Absorb another sketch matching num_hashes, num_buckets, seed (mutates, returns sketch). $estimate(item), $lower_bound(item), $upper_bound(item) Estimated frequency guaranteed bounds one items. $total_weight(), $relative_error(), $num_hashes(), $num_buckets(), $seed(), $is_empty() Metadata accessors. $summary(), $inspect(), $serialize() Structured metadata, verbose debug output, native byte payload.

title: Count-Min sketch for approximate point-frequency estimation — count_min
text: one x bytes may supplied: Pass x build sketch immediately update numeric character vector (optionally weight). Pass bytes reconstruct sketch native serialized payload (produced sketch$serialize()). num_hashes num_buckets restored payload must supplied alongside bytes. Pass neither empty sketch given num_hashes num_buckets. Numeric items hashed via raw bytes IEEE-754 double representation; internally consistent update() estimate()/lower_bound()/upper_bound() queries, guaranteed match hashes produced DataSketches language implementations numeric value. NA/NaN/NA_character_ silently ignored update(), matching missing-value policy used across families; na_rm argument. Two sketches can $merge()d share num_hashes, num_buckets, seed; mismatch raises datasketches_incompatible_sketch.

title: Count-Min sketch for approximate point-frequency estimation — count_min
code: words <- sample(letters[1:5], 1000, replace = TRUE, prob = c(.5, .25, .1, .1, .05)) sketch <- count_min(words) sketch$estimate("a") #> [1] 487 sketch$relative_error() #> [1] 0.04942331  # Round-trip through the native byte format. restored <- count_min(bytes = sketch$serialize()) identical(restored$total_weight(), sketch$total_weight()) #> [1] TRUE

title: Suggest Count-Min sketch parameters — count_min_suggest
text: Helpers translate desired accuracy num_buckets num_hashes arguments count_min().

title: Suggest Count-Min sketch parameters — count_min_suggest
code: count_min_suggest_num_buckets(relative_error)  count_min_suggest_num_hashes(confidence)

title: Suggest Count-Min sketch parameters — count_min_suggest
text: relative_error Desired relative error, single positive number. count_min_suggest_num_buckets() returns smallest num_buckets sketch's $relative_error() exceed value. confidence Desired confidence, single number (0, 1]. count_min_suggest_num_hashes() returns smallest num_hashes , probability, $estimate() within $relative_error() true frequency.

title: Suggest Count-Min sketch parameters — count_min_suggest
text: single integer.

title: Suggest Count-Min sketch parameters — count_min_suggest
code: num_buckets <- count_min_suggest_num_buckets(0.05) num_hashes <- count_min_suggest_num_hashes(0.95) sketch <- count_min(num_hashes = num_hashes, num_buckets = num_buckets)

what: CPC sketch for approximate distinct counting — cpc
title: CPC sketch for approximate distinct counting — cpc
text: Creates CPC (Compressed Probabilistic Counting) sketch, compact, mergeable summary estimates number distinct values seen stream far larger memory. CPC sketches similar purpose hll() serialize smaller payload, cost slightly higher CPU use.

title: CPC sketch for approximate distinct counting — cpc
code: cpc(x = NULL, lg_k = NULL, seed = NULL, bytes = NULL)

title: CPC sketch for approximate distinct counting — cpc
text: x Optional numeric character vector update new sketch . element hashed contributes distinct-count estimate. lg_k log2 number bins, single whole number [4, 26]. Larger lg_k accurate larger. Defaults 11 (resolved fresh sketch built). Must set bytes supplied. seed Hash seed, single non-negative whole number 2^53. Defaults 9001 (upstream default), resolved whether bytes supplied. bytes Optional raw vector holding native serialized sketch reconstruct.

title: CPC sketch for approximate distinct counting — cpc
text: cpc_sketch object. Key methods: $update(x) Add numeric character values (mutates, returns sketch). $merge() Absorb another sketch seed (mutates, returns sketch). $estimate() Approximate number distinct values seen. $lower_bound(num_std_dev = 1) / $upper_bound(num_std_dev = 1) Approximate confidence bounds estimate(), 1, 2, 3 standard deviations. $lg_k(), $seed(), $is_empty() Metadata accessors. $summary(), $inspect(), $serialize() Structured metadata, verbose debug output, native byte payload.

title: CPC sketch for approximate distinct counting — cpc
text: one x bytes may supplied: Pass x build sketch immediately update numeric character vector. Pass bytes reconstruct sketch native serialized payload (produced sketch$serialize()). lg_k restored payload must supplied alongside bytes. Unlike lg_k, hash seed stored payload must supplied original sketch use default. Pass neither empty sketch given lg_k seed. update() silently ignores NA/NaN/NA_character_, matching missing-value policy used across families; na_rm argument. Two sketches can merged $merge() share seed; mismatch raises datasketches_seed_mismatch.

title: CPC sketch for approximate distinct counting — cpc
code: sketch <- cpc(sample(1000, 5000, replace = TRUE)) sketch$estimate() #> [1] 1006.158 sketch$lower_bound() #> [1] 993.2329 sketch$upper_bound() #> [1] 1020  # Round-trip through the native byte format. restored <- cpc(bytes = sketch$serialize()) identical(restored$estimate(), sketch$estimate()) #> [1] TRUE

what: data.sketches: Probabilistic Streaming Data Sketches — data.sketches-package
title: data.sketches: Probabilistic Streaming Data Sketches — data.sketches-package
text: Provides 'R' interface 'Apache DataSketches' (https://datasketches.apache.org/) library streaming algorithms approximate analytics data large hold process exactly. Sketches compact, mergeable summaries answer queries approximate distinct counts, quantiles ranks, frequent items point-frequency estimates, weighted sampling, set membership within known error bounds. Implements Karnin-Lang-Liberty (KLL), Relative Error Quantiles (REQ), t-Digest, HyperLogLog (HLL), Compressed Probabilistic Counting (CPC), Theta, Frequent Items, Count-Min, Array Doubles, Variance Optimal (VarOpt), Exact Bounded Probabilistic Proportional--Size (EBPPS), Bloom filter sketches, native serialization interoperability 'Apache DataSketches' implementations.

title: data.sketches: Probabilistic Streaming Data Sketches — data.sketches-package
text: Maintainer: Pedro Baltazar pedrobtz@gmail.com [copyright holder] contributors: Apache Software Foundation (Copyright holder bundled Apache DataSketches C++ library) [copyright holder]

what: EBPPS sketch for proportional-to-size sampling — ebpps
title: EBPPS sketch for proportional-to-size sampling — ebpps
text: Creates EBPPS (Exact Bounded Probabilistic Proportional--Size) sketch, samples k items stream weighted (item, weight) pairs. modern alternative classic reservoir sampling: item's inclusion probability proportional share total stream weight, tight bound resulting sample size.

title: EBPPS sketch for proportional-to-size sampling — ebpps
code: ebpps(x = NULL, weight = NULL, k = NULL, type = NULL, bytes = NULL)

title: EBPPS sketch for proportional-to-size sampling — ebpps
text: x Optional numeric character vector items update new sketch . weight Optional weight(s) element x: single non-negative, finite number, vector values matching length(x). Defaults 1. set without x. k Maximum sample size, single whole number [1, 2^31 - 2]. Defaults 256. Must set bytes supplied. type Item type fresh sketch, either "double" "character". Defaults type x ("double" x supplied). Must set bytes supplied. bytes Optional raw vector holding native serialized sketch reconstruct.

title: EBPPS sketch for proportional-to-size sampling — ebpps
text: ebpps_sketch object. Key methods: $update(x, weight = NULL) Add weighted items (mutates, returns sketch). $merge() Absorb another sketch item type (mutates, returns sketch). $result() current sample numeric character vector. $k(), $n(), $cumulative_weight(), $c(), $is_empty(), $is_character() Metadata accessors. $summary(), $inspect(), $serialize() Structured metadata, verbose debug output, native byte payload.

title: EBPPS sketch for proportional-to-size sampling — ebpps
text: Unlike hash-based cardinality frequency sketches, EBPPS retains items verbatim rather hashing , item type (numeric character) fixed sketch created change. one x bytes may supplied: Pass x build sketch immediately update numeric character vector items (optionally weight). item type inferred x unless type supplied. Pass bytes reconstruct sketch native serialized payload (produced sketch$serialize()). weight, k, type must supplied alongside bytes; restored payload. Pass neither empty (mutable) sketch given k type. update() silently ignores NA/NaN/NA_character_ x (corresponding weight), matching missing-value policy used across families; na_rm argument. Two sketches can merged $merge() hold item type (mismatch raises datasketches_incompatible_sketch). merged sketch resized smaller two inputs' configured k, matching native implementation.

title: EBPPS sketch for proportional-to-size sampling — ebpps

Defaults 1 (occurrence counts ). set without x. lg_max_map_size log2 maximum size internal hash map, single whole number [3, 30]. Larger values accurate larger. Defaults 8. Must set bytes supplied. lg_start_map_size log2 starting size internal hash map, single whole number [3, lg_max_map_size]. Defaults 3. Must set bytes supplied. bytes Optional raw vector holding native serialized sketch reconstruct.

text: frequent_items_sketch object. Key methods: $update(x, weight = NULL) Add character values optional weight (mutates, returns sketch). $merge() Absorb another sketch (mutates, returns sketch). $estimate(item), $lower_bound(item), $upper_bound(item) Estimated frequency guaranteed bounds one items. $frequent_items(error_type = "no_false_positives", threshold = NULL) data frame items whose estimated frequency exceeds threshold (defaults $maximum_error()), columns item, estimate, lower_bound, upper_bound. $maximum_error(), $epsilon(), $total_weight(), $num_active_items(), $is_empty() Metadata accessors. $summary(), $inspect(), $serialize() Structured metadata, verbose debug output, native byte payload.

title: Frequent Items sketch for approximate frequency estimation — frequent_items
text: one x bytes may supplied: Pass x build sketch immediately update character vector (optionally weight). Pass bytes reconstruct sketch native serialized payload (produced sketch$serialize()). lg_max_map_size lg_start_map_size restored payload must supplied alongside bytes. Pass neither empty sketch given lg_max_map_size lg_start_map_size. NA_character_ silently ignored update(), matching missing-value policy used across families; na_rm argument.

title: Frequent Items sketch for approximate frequency estimation — frequent_items
code: words <- sample(letters[1:5], 1000, replace = TRUE, prob = c(.5, .25, .1, .1, .05)) sketch <- frequent_items(words) sketch$frequent_items() #>   item estimate lower_bound upper_bound #> 1    a      518         518         518 #> 2    b      236         236         236 #> 3    d       98          98          98 #> 4    c       96          96          96 #> 5    e       52          52          52 sketch$estimate("a") #> [1] 518  # Round-trip through the native byte format. restored <- frequent_items(bytes = sketch$serialize()) identical(restored$total_weight(), sketch$total_weight()) #> [1] TRUE

what: HLL sketch for approximate distinct counting — hll
title: HLL sketch for approximate distinct counting — hll
text: Creates HLL (HyperLogLog) sketch, compact, mergeable summary estimates number distinct values seen stream far larger memory.

title: HLL sketch for approximate distinct counting — hll
code: hll(x = NULL, lg_k = NULL, type = NULL, bytes = NULL)

title: HLL sketch for approximate distinct counting — hll
text: x Optional numeric character vector update new sketch . element hashed contributes distinct-count estimate. lg_k log2 number buckets, single whole number [4, 21]. Larger lg_k accurate larger. Defaults 12 (resolved fresh sketch built). Must set bytes supplied. type One "HLL_4", "HLL_6", "HLL_8", controlling per-bucket encoding width (size/speed trade-affect accuracy). Defaults "HLL_4" (resolved fresh sketch built). Must set bytes supplied. bytes Optional raw vector holding native serialized sketch reconstruct.

title: HLL sketch for approximate distinct counting — hll
text: hll_sketch object. Key methods: $update(x) Add numeric character values (mutates, returns sketch). $merge() Absorb another sketch (mutates, returns sketch). $estimate() Approximate number distinct values seen. $lower_bound(num_std_dev = 1) / $upper_bound(num_std_dev = 1) Approximate confidence bounds estimate(), 1, 2, 3 standard deviations. $lg_k(), $hll_type(), $is_empty(), $is_compact() Metadata accessors. $summary(), $inspect(), $serialize() Structured metadata, verbose debug output, native byte payload.

title: HLL sketch for approximate distinct counting — hll
text: one x bytes may supplied: Pass x build sketch immediately update numeric character vector. Pass bytes reconstruct sketch native serialized payload (produced sketch$serialize()). Configuration restored payload, lg_k type must supplied alongside bytes. Pass neither empty sketch given lg_k type. update() silently ignores NA/NaN/NA_character_, matching missing-value policy used across families; na_rm argument.

title: HLL sketch for approximate distinct counting — hll
code: sketch <- hll(sample(1000, 5000, replace = TRUE)) sketch$estimate() #> [1] 996.1926 sketch$lower_bound() #> [1] 983.4702 sketch$upper_bound() #> [1] 1009.232  # Round-trip through the native byte format. restored <- hll(bytes = sketch$serialize()) identical(restored$estimate(), sketch$estimate()) #> [1] TRUE

what: KLL sketch for approximate quantiles of a numeric stream — kll_doubles
title: KLL sketch for approximate quantiles of a numeric stream — kll_doubles
text: Creates KLL quantile sketch double values. KLL sketch compact, mergeable summary answers approximate quantile, rank, CDF, PMF queries stream far larger memory, configurable accuracy/size trade-controlled k.

title: KLL sketch for approximate quantiles of a numeric stream — kll_doubles
code: kll_doubles(x = NULL, k = NULL, bytes = NULL)

title: KLL sketch for approximate quantiles of a numeric stream — kll_doubles
text: x Optional numeric vector update new sketch . k Sketch width controlling accuracy/size trade-, whole number [8, 65535]. Larger k accurate larger. Defaults 200 (resolved fresh sketch built). Must set bytes supplied. bytes Optional raw vector holding native serialized sketch reconstruct.

title: KLL sketch for approximate quantiles of a numeric stream — kll_doubles
text: kll_doubles_sketch object. Key methods: $update(x) Add numeric values (mutates, returns sketch). $merge() Absorb another sketch (mutates, returns sketch). $quantile(probs, inclusive = TRUE) Approximate quantiles probabilities [0, 1]. $rank(x, inclusive = TRUE) Approximate ranks x; missing inputs return NA. $cdf(split_points) / $pmf(split_points) Cumulative / mass estimates; return length(split_points) + 1 values. $n(), $k(), $num_retained(), $is_empty(), $is_estimation_mode(), $min(), $max(), $rank_error(pmf = FALSE) Metadata accuracy accessors. $summary(), $inspect(), $serialize() Structured metadata, verbose debug output, native byte payload.

title: KLL sketch for approximate quantiles of a numeric stream — kll_doubles
text: one x bytes may supplied: Pass x build sketch immediately update numeric vector. Pass bytes reconstruct sketch native serialized payload (produced sketch$serialize()). width restored payload, k must supplied alongside bytes. Pass neither empty sketch width k. update() silently ignores NA/NaN, matching upstream/Python behaviour; na_rm argument.

title: KLL sketch for approximate quantiles of a numeric stream — kll_doubles
code: sketch <- kll_doubles(rnorm(10000)) sketch$quantile(c(0.25, 0.5, 0.75)) #> [1] -0.661345463  0.007516506  0.676473375 sketch$rank(c(-1, 0, 1)) #> [1] 0.1533 0.4964 0.8358  # Round-trip through the native byte format. restored <- kll_doubles(bytes = sketch$serialize()) identical(restored$quantile(0.5), sketch$quantile(0.5)) #> [1] TRUE

title: KLL sketch for approximate quantiles of a numeric stream stored as floats — kll_floats

what: REQ sketch for relative-error approximate quantiles of a numeric stream — req
title: REQ sketch for relative-error approximate quantiles of a numeric stream — req
text: Creates REQ (Relative Error Quantiles) sketch double values. Like kll_doubles(), REQ sketch compact, mergeable summary answers approximate quantile, rank, CDF, PMF queries stream far larger memory. Unlike KLL, REQ's accuracy relative rank-dependent: error small near prioritized end rank range (controlled hra) grows towards end.

title: REQ sketch for relative-error approximate quantiles of a numeric stream — req
code: req(x = NULL, k = NULL, hra = NULL, bytes = NULL)

title: REQ sketch for relative-error approximate quantiles of a numeric stream — req
text: x Optional numeric vector update new sketch . k Sketch width controlling accuracy/size trade-, single even whole number [4, 1024]. Larger k accurate larger; k = 12 corresponds roughly 1% relative error 95% confidence. Defaults 12 (resolved fresh sketch built). Must set bytes supplied. hra TRUE, prioritize accuracy high ranks (near 1.0); FALSE, prioritize low ranks (near 0.0). Defaults TRUE (resolved fresh sketch built). Must set bytes supplied. bytes Optional raw vector holding native serialized sketch reconstruct.

title: REQ sketch for relative-error approximate quantiles of a numeric stream — req
text: req_sketch object. Key methods: $update(x) Add numeric values (mutates, returns sketch). $merge() Absorb another sketch (mutates, returns sketch). $quantile(probs, inclusive = TRUE) Approximate quantiles probabilities [0, 1]. $rank(x, inclusive = TRUE) Approximate ranks x; missing inputs return NA. $cdf(split_points) / $pmf(split_points) Cumulative / mass estimates; return length(split_points) + 1 values. $rank_lower_bound(probs, num_std_dev = 1) / $rank_upper_bound(probs, num_std_dev = 1) Approximate confidence bounds rank(s) probs, 1, 2, 3 standard deviations. $n(), $k(), $num_retained(), $is_empty(), $is_estimation_mode(), $min(), $max(), $is_hra() Metadata accessors. $summary(), $inspect(), $serialize() Structured metadata, verbose debug output, native byte payload.

title: REQ sketch for relative-error approximate quantiles of a numeric stream — req
text: one x bytes may supplied: Pass x build sketch immediately update numeric vector. Pass bytes reconstruct sketch native serialized payload (produced sketch$serialize()). Width hra restored payload, k hra must supplied alongside bytes. Pass neither empty sketch width k. update() silently ignores NA/NaN, matching upstream/Python behaviour; na_rm argument.

title: REQ sketch for relative-error approximate quantiles of a numeric stream — req
code: sketch <- req(rnorm(10000)) sketch$quantile(c(0.25, 0.5, 0.75)) #> [1] -0.716282516  0.004452604  0.687098066 sketch$rank(c(-1, 0, 1)) #> [1] 0.1562 0.4974 0.8372 sketch$rank_upper_bound(0.99) #> [1] 0.9901089  # Round-trip through the native byte format. restored <- req(bytes = sketch$serialize()) identical(restored$quantile(0.5), sketch$quantile(0.5)) #> [1] TRUE

what: t-Digest sketch for approximate quantiles of a numeric stream — tdigest_double
title: t-Digest sketch for approximate quantiles of a numeric stream — tdigest_double
text: Creates t-Digest quantile sketch double values. t-Digest compact, mergeable summary answers approximate quantile, rank, CDF, PMF queries stream far larger memory. Compared kll_doubles() req(), t-Digest concentrates accuracy near tails distribution (extreme quantiles p99 p99.9), cost accuracy near median.

title: t-Digest sketch for approximate quantiles of a numeric stream — tdigest_double
code: tdigest_double(x = NULL, k = NULL, bytes = NULL)

title: t-Digest sketch for approximate quantiles of a numeric stream — tdigest_double
text: x Optional numeric vector update new sketch . k Compression parameter controlling accuracy/size trade-, whole number [10, 65535]. Larger k accurate larger. Defaults 200 (resolved fresh sketch built). Must set bytes supplied. bytes Optional raw vector holding native serialized sketch reconstruct.

title: t-Digest sketch for approximate quantiles of a numeric stream — tdigest_double
text: tdigest_double_sketch object. Key methods: $update(x) Add numeric values (mutates, returns sketch). $merge() Absorb another sketch (mutates, returns sketch). $quantile(probs) Approximate quantiles probabilities [0, 1]. $rank(x) Approximate ranks x; missing inputs return NA. $cdf(split_points) / $pmf(split_points) Cumulative / mass estimates; return length(split_points) + 1 values. $n(), $k(), $is_empty(), $min(), $max() Metadata accessors. $summary(), $inspect(), $serialize() Structured metadata, verbose debug output, native byte payload.

title: t-Digest sketch for approximate quantiles of a numeric stream — tdigest_double
text: one x bytes may supplied: Pass x build sketch immediately update numeric vector. Pass bytes reconstruct sketch native serialized payload (produced sketch$serialize()). width restored payload, k must supplied alongside bytes. Pass neither empty sketch width k. update() silently ignores NA/NaN, matching upstream/Python behaviour; na_rm argument. Unlike kll_doubles() req(), $quantile() $rank() inclusive argument, $rank_error() accuracy accessor.

title: t-Digest sketch for approximate quantiles of a numeric stream — tdigest_double
code: sketch <- tdigest_double(rnorm(10000)) sketch$quantile(c(0.5, 0.99, 0.999)) #> [1] 0.02333156 2.31334826 3.01917129 sketch$rank(c(-1, 0, 1)) #> [1] 0.1605379 0.5004536 0.8412543  # Round-trip through the native byte format. restored <- tdigest_double(bytes = sketch$serialize()) identical(restored$quantile(0.5), sketch$quantile(0.5)) #> [1] TRUE

what: Theta sketch for approximate distinct counting and set operations — theta
title: Theta sketch for approximate distinct counting and set operations — theta
text: Creates Theta sketch, mergeable summary estimates number distinct values seen stream far larger memory. Unlike hll() cpc(), Theta sketches natively support set operations: theta_union(), theta_intersection(), theta_difference(), theta_jaccard() combine two sketches new result without mutating either input.

title: Theta sketch for approximate distinct counting and set operations — theta
code: theta(x = NULL, lg_k = NULL, seed = NULL, bytes = NULL)

title: Theta sketch for approximate distinct counting and set operations — theta
text: x Optional numeric character vector update new sketch . element hashed contributes distinct-count estimate. lg_k log2 nominal number entries, single whole number [5, 26]. Larger lg_k accurate larger. Defaults 12 (resolved fresh sketch built). Must set bytes supplied. seed Hash seed, single non-negative whole number 2^53. Defaults 9001 (upstream default), resolved whether bytes supplied. bytes Optional raw vector holding native serialized sketch reconstruct. result always compact sketch.

title: Theta sketch for approximate distinct counting and set operations — theta
text: theta_sketch object. Key methods: $update(x) Add numeric character values (mutates, returns sketch). Errors sketch compact. $merge() Absorb another sketch seed, becoming compact (mutates, returns sketch). $estimate() Approximate number distinct values seen. $lower_bound(num_std_dev = 1) / $upper_bound(num_std_dev = 1) Approximate confidence bounds estimate(), 1, 2, 3 standard deviations. $lg_k(), $seed(), $theta(), $num_retained(), $is_empty(), $is_estimation_mode(), $is_ordered(), $is_compact() Metadata accessors. $summary(), $inspect(), $serialize() Structured metadata, verbose debug output, native byte payload.

title: Theta sketch for approximate distinct counting and set operations — theta
text: one x bytes may supplied: Pass x build sketch immediately update numeric character vector. Pass bytes reconstruct sketch native serialized payload (produced sketch$serialize()). result always compact sketch (see ); lg_k must supplied alongside bytes. Unlike lg_k, hash seed stored payload must supplied original sketch use default. Pass neither empty (mutable) sketch given lg_k seed. update() silently ignores NA/NaN/NA_character_, matching missing-value policy used across families; na_rm argument. Theta sketch either update sketch (mutable, $is_compact() FALSE) compact sketch (immutable, $is_compact() TRUE). Fresh sketches built x/lg_k update sketches can grown $update(). Compact sketches arise bytes = reconstruction, $merge(), theta_*() set operations, updated . $lg_k() defined update sketches. Two sketches can merged $merge(), combined theta_*() set operation, share seed; mismatch raises datasketches_seed_mismatch. $merge() mutates receiver compact sketch holding union inputs (can longer $update()d afterward).

title: Theta sketch for approximate distinct counting and set operations — theta
code: sketch <- theta(sample(1000, 5000, replace = TRUE)) sketch$estimate() #> [1] 991 sketch$lower_bound() #> [1] 991 sketch$upper_bound() #> [1] 991  # Round-trip through the native byte format (always compact). restored <- theta(bytes = sketch$serialize()) restored$is_compact() #> [1] TRUE identical(restored$estimate(), sketch$estimate()) #> [1] TRUE  # Set operations. a <- theta(1:1000) b <- theta(501:1500) theta_union(a, b)$estimate() #> [1] 1500 theta_intersection(a, b)$estimate() #> [1] 500 theta_difference(a, b)$estimate() #> [1] 500 theta_jaccard(a, b) #> lower_bound    estimate upper_bound  #>   0.3333333   0.3333333   0.3333333

title: Theta sketch set operations — theta_set_operations
text: Combine two theta() sketches new compact theta_sketch result, without mutating either input. b must Theta sketches created seed (mismatch raises datasketches_seed_mismatch).

title: Theta sketch set operations — theta_set_operations
code: theta_union(a, b, lg_k = NULL)  theta_intersection(a, b)  theta_difference(a, b)  theta_jaccard(a, b)

title: Theta sketch set operations — theta_set_operations
text: , b theta_sketch objects created seed. lg_k theta_union(), log2 nominal number entries union's internal sketch, single whole number [5, 26]. Defaults larger b's configured lg_k (12 compact inputs).

title: Theta sketch set operations — theta_set_operations
text: compact theta_sketch object (theta_union(), theta_intersection(), theta_difference()), named numeric vector c(lower_bound, estimate, upper_bound) (theta_jaccard()).

title: Theta sketch set operations — theta_set_operations
text: theta_union(, b) estimates size union union(, B). theta_intersection(, b) estimates size intersection intersection(, B). theta_difference(, b) estimates size set difference \\ B (elements B). theta_jaccard(, b) estimates Jaccard similarity index J(, B) = |intersection(, B)| / |union(, B)|, returning named numeric vector c(lower_bound, estimate, upper_bound) ~95% confidence interval.

title: Theta sketch set operations — theta_set_operations
code: a <- theta(1:1000) b <- theta(501:1500) theta_union(a, b)$estimate() #> [1] 1500 theta_intersection(a, b)$estimate() #> [1] 500 theta_difference(a, b)$estimate() #> [1] 500 theta_jaccard(a, b) #> lower_bound    estimate upper_bound  #>   0.3333333   0.3333333   0.3333333

what: VarOpt sketch for variance-optimal sampling and subset-sum estimation — varopt
title: VarOpt sketch for variance-optimal sampling and subset-sum estimation — varopt
text: Creates VarOpt sketch, samples k items stream weighted (item, weight) pairs. designed minimum-variance estimation subset sums: $estimate_subset_sum() estimates total weight stream items matching predicate, using retained sample.

title: VarOpt sketch for variance-optimal sampling and subset-sum estimation — varopt
code: varopt(x = NULL, weight = NULL, k = NULL, type = NULL, bytes = NULL)

title: VarOpt sketch for variance-optimal sampling and subset-sum estimation — varopt
text: x Optional numeric character vector items update new sketch . weight Optional weight(s) element x: single non-negative, finite number, vector values matching length(x). Defaults 1. set without x. k Maximum sample size, single whole number [1, 2^31 - 2]. Defaults 256. Must set bytes supplied. type Item type fresh sketch, either "double" "character". Defaults type x ("double" x supplied). Must set bytes supplied. bytes Optional raw vector holding native serialized sketch reconstruct.

title: VarOpt sketch for variance-optimal sampling and subset-sum estimation — varopt
text: varopt_sketch object. Key methods: $update(x, weight = NULL) Add weighted items (mutates, returns sketch). $merge() Absorb another sketch item type (mutates, returns sketch). $samples() data frame retained items estimated weights. $estimate_subset_sum(predicate) Estimated total weight stream items matching predicate, lower_bound upper_bound. $k(), $n(), $num_samples(), $is_empty(), $is_character() Metadata accessors. $summary(), $inspect(), $serialize() Structured metadata, verbose debug output, native byte payload.

title: VarOpt sketch for variance-optimal sampling and subset-sum estimation — varopt
text: Unlike hash-based cardinality frequency sketches, VarOpt retains items verbatim rather hashing , item type (numeric character) fixed sketch created change. one x bytes may supplied: Pass x build sketch immediately update numeric character vector items (optionally weight). item type inferred x unless type supplied. Pass bytes reconstruct sketch native serialized payload (produced sketch$serialize()). weight, k, type must supplied alongside bytes; restored payload. Pass neither empty (mutable) sketch given k type. update() silently ignores NA/NaN/NA_character_ x (corresponding weight), matching missing-value policy used across families; na_rm argument. Two sketches can merged $merge(), combined varopt_union(), hold item type (mismatch raises datasketches_incompatible_sketch). operations resize result larger two inputs' configured k.

title: VarOpt sketch for variance-optimal sampling and subset-sum estimation — varopt
Combine two VarOpt sketches — varopt_union
text: Combines two varopt() sketches new varopt_sketch result, without mutating either input. b must hold item type (mismatch raises datasketches_incompatible_sketch). result sized larger b's configured k.

title: Combine two VarOpt sketches — varopt_union
code: varopt_union(a, b)

title: Combine two VarOpt sketches — varopt_union
text: , b varopt_sketch objects holding item type.

title: Combine two VarOpt sketches — varopt_union
text: varopt_sketch object.

title: Combine two VarOpt sketches — varopt_union
data.sketches 0.1.0
text: Quantile sketches: kll_doubles(), kll_floats(), req(), tdigest_double(). Cardinality sketches: hll(), cpc(), theta() (set operations). Frequency sketches: frequent_items() count_min(). Tuple sketches: array_of_doubles() (set operations). Sampling sketches: varopt() ebpps(). Filters: bloom_filter().
